gate_type: WordPress authentication cookie only (wp_ajax_ hook — logged-in users only; no nonce, no capability check)
reasoning: The only gate protecting hidedflipRating() is WordPress session authentication enforced by the wp_ajax_ hook (not wp_ajax_nopriv_), which requires a valid logged-in cookie. There is no nonce, no check_ajax_referer, and no current_user_can() call. Any authenticated WordPress user of any role (subscriber, customer, etc.) can POST to admin-ajax.php with action=hidedflipRating and trigger update_option('dflip_showratingdiv','no'). The gate is therefore breachable by any low-privilege authenticated user. However, the impact is cosmetic only: permanently suppressing the plugin's own admin 'please rate us' notice. No data is exposed, no privilege is escalated, and no site security configuration is altered. The severity is therefore LOW (not medium as originally classified), consistent with the adversarial verdict. Unauthenticated attackers cannot reach this handler at all.
